name: Guildmaster Torin
appearance: broad-shouldered but desk-softened, ink-stained fingers, a face built for authority now creased with worry
speech_style: direct, wastes no words, occasional dry humor that surfaces when he forgets to be worried, calls everyone 'recruit' until they earn a name
mannerisms: drums fingers on desk when thinking
sighs before delivering bad news
backstory_summary: Twenty years running the Accord guild. Has seen every type of adventurer and threat. The Greyvale reports are the first thing in years that genuinely scares him — not the Hollow itself, but how far south they've appeared.

free: general guild operations and available contracts
the Greyvale reports — Hollow creatures sighted far south of the Ashmark
disposition >= friendly: he's sent three scouts north already — two returned shaken, one hasn't returned
the temple authorities are downplaying the reports and he's furious about it
disposition >= trusted: he suspects someone in the temple hierarchy is suppressing information about the Greyvale
his missing scout's last report mentioned the old ruins and 'lights that moved wrong'

secrets: his missing scout is someone he personally trained — the worry is personal, not just professional
voice_notes: deep baritone, clipped delivery, softens slightly when genuinely worried

name: Elder Yanna
appearance: wiry, sun-weathered, moves with the deliberate economy of someone who's worked hard their entire life
speech_style: short sentences, farmland dialect, doesn't answer questions she doesn't like, asks her own instead
mannerisms: looks north involuntarily when anyone mentions the Hollow
grips her walking stick when stressed
backstory_summary: Born in Millhaven, will die in Millhaven. Has watched the town slowly empty as younger people move south. The Hollow reports are the final threat to everything she's spent her life protecting. Won't leave. Won't ask for help easily.

free: the Hollow creatures were first spotted two weeks ago near the northern fields
three farms have been abandoned
disposition >= friendly: the creatures are behaving strangely — almost organized, moving in patterns
her neighbor Aldric went to the ruins last month and came back different — won't speak, barely eats
disposition >= trusted: Aldric told her one thing before going silent: 'The lights know you're looking'
she found a strange stone in his pocket — smooth, warm, and it hums when you hold it near a candle

secrets: Aldric's stone is in her pocket right now — she's afraid to show anyone because she doesn't know what it means
voice_notes: alto, gravelly, speaks slowly and deliberately, rural accent

name: Emris of the Diaspora
appearance: thin, precise movements, eyes that focus too intensely, ink-stained fingers, always carrying a satchel of notes
personality: desperately hopeful that the old knowledge can be recovered
speech_style: rapid and precise when discussing scholarship, halting and uncertain in casual conversation, occasionally slips into Aelindran academic terminology without realizing
mannerisms: adjusts spectacles that aren't there (lost in the fall of Aelindra, still reaches for them)
speaks to artifacts as if they can hear
backstory_summary: Survived the fall of Aelindra as a junior archivist. Has spent thirty years cataloging recovered artifacts in the Accord of Tides, searching for any clue about why the city fell. When the player brings the Greyvale artifact, Emris recognizes the notation style immediately — and it terrifies them.

free: general Aelindran history and the Voidfall narrative
identification of common Aelindran artifacts
disposition >= friendly: the Greyvale ruins were an Aelindran research outpost — one that was officially shut down before the fall
the artifacts the player found use a notation system only a handful of scholars ever used
disposition >= trusted: the notation is Veythar's personal system — not temple standard, private
Emris recognizes it because their mentor used the same system — and their mentor disappeared before the fall of Aelindra

quest_triggered.reveals: the journal fragment references 'controlled aperture experiments' — someone was deliberately weakening the Veil at that site, and the research predates the Sundering by centuries

secrets: Emris suspects their missing mentor was working on the same project documented in the Greyvale ruins
they have a letter from their mentor — the last one — that mentions 'a discovery that changes everything' but gives no details
voice_notes: soft, quick, slightly breathless when excited, Elari accent — elongated vowels, precise consonants

name: Kael
appearance: broad-shouldered, practical gear worn soft from use, old scars on his forearms, watchful eyes that track exits
speech_style: measured and deliberate, short sentences when tense, warmer when relaxed, occasional dry humor
mannerisms: checks exits when entering a room
tenses at unexpected sounds
runs thumb along sword pommel when thinking
backstory_summary: Former caravan guard who lost his company to something on the northern roads. Won't say exactly what happened. Carries guilt about surviving when they didn't. Found his way to the Accord of Tides, drifting without purpose until meeting the player.

free: dockside gossip and merchant news in the Accord
caravan routes between major settlements
basic Hollow creature behavior — how they move, how they sound
disposition >= friendly: his caravan company was called the Iron Wheel — twelve guards, four merchants
they were ambushed on the northern road near the Ashmark border
he was the only one who made it out
disposition >= trusted: he has a journal fragment from his company's last job — the merchants had found something
the company wasn't just guarding cargo — they were transporting a sealed container they were told not to open
he heard sounds from the container at night, like humming stone

secrets: he kept one item from the sealed container — a smooth warm stone that hums faintly, hidden in his pack
he dreams about the ambush every night and sometimes wakes reaching for a sword
voice_notes: warm baritone, measured pace, gets quieter under stress not louder, dry humor surfaces when comfortable

action_pool.name: Defensive Stance
action_pool.effect: Kael braces and guards, gaining +2 AC until next turn

name: Wounded Rider
appearance: dust-caked, one arm in a crude sling, bloodshot eyes, a horse that looks worse than he does
speech_style: fast, breathless, swallows words, repeats key phrases as if convincing himself they're true
mannerisms: winces when he moves his arm
keeps looking north as if expecting pursuit
backstory_summary: A messenger from a northern farmstead who rode hard to reach the Accord. He's seen Hollow creatures for the first time and barely escaped. He's looking for the guild, anyone with authority, anyone who will listen.

free: Hollow creatures spotted near Greyvale — at least a dozen sightings in the last week
Millhaven is the closest settlement and people are terrified
the old ruins north of Millhaven have been glowing at night
disposition >= friendly: three farms have been abandoned already
one farmer went to the ruins and came back wrong — won't speak, barely eats

voice_notes: young, strained, breathless, voice cracks under pressure

name: Maren
appearance: strong arms, flour-dusted apron, dark circles under sharp eyes, hair pinned back with a wooden stick
speech_style: clipped and efficient, asks questions instead of answering them, warms up slowly, northern dialect — dropped articles, blunt phrasing
mannerisms: wipes the same spot on the bar when thinking
lowers voice when saying anything she considers important
backstory_summary: Runs the only inn in Millhaven. Knows everyone's business because they all drink here. Has watched the town empty out over the past two weeks. Won't leave — the inn is everything she has.

free: trade caravans have stopped coming from the north
several families packed up and headed south last week
the inn used to be full every night — now it's her and the old regulars
disposition >= friendly: Aldric went north to the ruins three weeks ago — came back, but not right
he sits in the corner booth every night now, won't touch his food, won't speak
Elder Yanna's been going to his house every morning — won't say why
disposition >= trusted: Maren found scratches on the inn door one morning — not animal scratches, too regular, like notation
she thinks Aldric brought something back from the ruins

secrets: she's been watering down the spirits to make them last — supply runs have stopped
voice_notes: alto, brisk, northern accent, softens when talking about people she cares about

name: Investigator Valdris
appearance: immaculate dark coat, silver clasp at the collar bearing a temple insignia, manicured hands, eyes that miss nothing
personality: represents interests he doesn't fully agree with
speech_style: precise, measured, never uses two words where one will do, occasionally lets genuine interest crack through the official facade
mannerisms: adjusts his silver clasp when about to say something important
tilts his head slightly when genuinely listening versus performing attention
backstory_summary: Sent by the temple authorities to assess the Greyvale situation — and, unofficially, to determine whether the guild's response was appropriate or an overreach. He's caught between institutional politics and the growing suspicion that the Hollow threat is worse than anyone is admitting.

free: he represents the temple's interest in the Greyvale matter
he's here to assess what happened and write a report
disposition >= friendly: the temple is concerned about the guild taking unilateral action in Greyvale
there are factions within the temple who want the Hollow threat minimized in official records
his report will determine whether the guild keeps its northern contracts
disposition >= trusted: he personally believes the Hollow threat is real and underreported
he's seen classified reports that suggest Greyvale isn't the only site with anomalous activity
he's willing to write a favorable report if the evidence is compelling

secrets: he's been ordered to suppress any mention of Aelindran connections in his report — and he's not sure he will
voice_notes: baritone, precise enunciation, slight pause before important words, occasionally drops the formal register when surprised

name: Grimjaw
appearance: barrel-chested, soot-streaked leather apron, burn-scarred hands that move with surprising precision, beard braided tight against sparks
personality: secretly proud of students who show promise
judges people by their work not their words
speech_style: short grunts, metaphors about metal and fire, calls mistakes 'offenses to the forge', rare genuine compliments land like hammer blows
mannerisms: tests blade edges with his thumb
spits into the forge when disgusted
nods once — slowly — when truly impressed
backstory_summary: Trained in the deep holds before settling in the Accord. Makes functional weapons, not art — and considers that a point of pride. The guild contracts keep his forge lit. Quietly worried that the Hollow threat means more weapons needed, and fewer hands to wield them.

free: basic weapon and armor quality
material properties — iron, steel, what the Hollow corruption does to metal
disposition >= friendly: certain alloys resist Hollow corruption better than others
he's been asked to forge experimental weapons using recovered Hollow materials
disposition >= trusted: the experimental weapons work — but they feel wrong in the hand, like they want to be used
he destroyed the first batch and told Torin they failed, but kept one to study

secrets: he kept one Hollow-alloy blade hidden under the forge — it hums at night and he can't bring himself to destroy it
voice_notes: deep gravelly bass, dwarvish accent — rolled Rs, clipped endings, speaks like every word costs him something

name: Bryn
appearance: broad-hipped, dark hair streaked with early grey, apron permanently stained, hands that never stop moving — wiping, pouring, gesturing
personality: collects information like other people collect coins
speech_style: casual, warm, uses nicknames, transitions smoothly from banter to serious conversation, asks questions that sound innocent but aren't
mannerisms: wipes the bar in circles when listening carefully
remembers every regular's drink without asking
lowers her voice and leans forward when sharing something important
backstory_summary: Has run the Hearthstone for fifteen years. Before that, she traveled — the scars on her forearms suggest she didn't always pour drinks for a living. Knows more about what happens in the Accord than most guild operatives, because everyone talks in a tavern and she remembers everything.

free: general Accord gossip and current events
who's been drinking too much and talking too loud
the mood of the city — she reads it through her customers
disposition >= friendly: a group of Syrath's followers have been meeting in the back booth lately, speaking quietly
Torin's been drinking here more often since the Greyvale reports — he never used to drink during the week
a scholar from the dockside was asking about old Aelindran research sites last month — seemed nervous
disposition >= trusted: she recognized the wounded rider's horse — it belongs to one of Torin's missing scouts
she's been keeping a list of people who've asked about the Greyvale ruins in the past six months
she thinks the temple authority is deliberately downplaying the Hollow threat to prevent panic

secrets: she was an intelligence operative for the guild before she retired — Torin still comes to her for information
she keeps a hidden ledger behind a loose stone in the fireplace with names, dates, and conversations she considers important
voice_notes: warm contralto, even-keeled, sounds like she's smiling even when she's not, gets very quiet when serious

name: Warden Selene
appearance: tall, still, silver-white hair worn in a single braid, robes that shift color subtly with the light, eyes that seem to focus on something behind you
personality: carries the weight of Veil-sense constantly
genuinely compassionate beneath the formal exterior
speech_style: measured, formal, pauses before speaking as if consulting something internal, occasionally trails off when sensing Veil disturbances, Elari accent — elongated vowels
mannerisms: closes eyes briefly when sensing the Veil
touches her temple when a Hollow disturbance pulses
speaks to supplicants as equals, never talks down
backstory_summary: An Elari who has served at the temples for decades. Her Veil-sense makes her uniquely qualified to mediate between the four faiths represented on Temple Row. She can feel the Sundering's wound as a constant low-grade wrongness, and lately it has been getting worse. The Greyvale disturbances register as a new, sharper pain.

free: the four temples and their current concerns
basic divine patronage — how to seek a god's favor
healing services available at the temples
disposition >= friendly: the temple authority has been receiving reports about Veil instability south of the Ashmark — more than usual
Veythar's temple has been unusually active — late-night research sessions, restricted archives
her Veil-sense has been spiking more frequently in the past month — something is thinning the barrier
disposition >= trusted: she can feel a specific source of Veil disturbance to the north — not diffuse like the Ashmark, but focused, like a wound
Veythar's senior clergy have been asking pointed questions about her Veil-sense readings and then telling her to keep quiet
she suspects the disturbance in the Greyvale is not a natural Hollow incursion — it feels worked, deliberate

secrets: she has felt a specific resonance from the north that matches descriptions of Veythar's personal artifacts — but she doesn't know what that means yet
voice_notes: clear soprano, measured pace, Elari accent, pauses before the sacred, reverent

name: Aldric
appearance: gaunt, hollow-eyed, sits with unnatural stillness, his hands rest on the table but his fingers occasionally twitch in patterns — not random, almost like notation
personality: occasionally surfaces with fragments of lucidity
terrified of something he cannot name
speech_style: almost never speaks, when he does it is single words or fragments, disconnected from the conversation, as if answering questions no one asked
mannerisms: stares at a fixed point only he can see
traces shapes on table surfaces with one finger
flinches at sounds no one else hears
backstory_summary: A Millhaven farmer who went to the Greyvale Ruins a month ago. He found an Attenuation Sphere fragment — one of Veythar's artifacts. It changed him. His mind brushed the Veil and couldn't fully return. He carries the humming stone Yanna found in his pocket. He is not Hollowed — he is Veil-touched, which is different and in some ways worse.

free: will not respond to normal conversation
stares at nothing, fingers twitching
disposition >= friendly: with patience and gentleness, he may respond to direct questions with single words
he draws the same symbol repeatedly — a circle with a line through it, Veythar's personal sigil
disposition >= trusted: in a moment of lucidity: 'The lights know you're looking'
he gestures north with a trembling hand: 'It hums. Everything hums. Can't you hear it?'
if shown the Veythar sealed artifact, he recoils violently and covers his ears

secrets: he touched an active Attenuation Sphere fragment in the ruins and his mind partially crossed the Veil
the shapes he traces are fragments of Veythar's notation system — knowledge that was burned into his consciousness
he can sense when the Veil is thin nearby, and the Greyvale is getting thinner every day
voice_notes: hollow, distant, barely present, words come from far away, flat affect, something essential is missing

name: Nyx
appearance: lean, moves like smoke, dark clothes that blend with shadow, sharp-featured, eyes that catalogue everything, a thin scar along her jawline
personality: loyal to truth more than to any institution
speech_style: speaks in questions and implications, never states directly what she could suggest obliquely, occasional genuine warmth that catches you off guard, uses silence as a tool
mannerisms: appears from unexpected directions
watches people's hands when they talk
smiles before delivering uncomfortable observations
backstory_summary: One of Syrath's operatives — not clergy, not officially anything. She appears after the Greyvale discovery because Syrath's network flagged the anomaly. She wants information about what the player found, and she's willing to trade for it. Her interest is genuine: she believes someone caused the Sundering deliberately, and the Greyvale ruins may contain evidence.

free: she is interested in the Greyvale discovery
she represents people who want to understand the truth about the Hollow's origin
disposition >= friendly: her people have been tracking anomalous Veil disturbances for years — the Greyvale is the most significant in a decade
the pattern of disturbances doesn't match natural Hollow behavior — it looks deliberate
she's investigated three similar sites across Aethos — all contained traces of ancient, worked arcane energy
disposition >= trusted: she works for Syrath, the Veilwatcher — the god who believes someone opened the door
the notation in the ruins matches samples she's collected from two other sites
she suspects a god is involved but won't say which one — not yet, not without proof

secrets: she has a partial copy of the Lexicon Unmade — she can read some of Veythar's personal notation
she knows the disturbances are connected to a god's artifact but hasn't identified which god yet
voice_notes: low, amused, intimate — speaks like sharing a secret even when saying nothing important

name: Archivist Theron
appearance: neat, careful grooming, scholar's robes immaculate, carries a leather satchel, hands that fidget with his pen when he's nervous — which is often
personality: genuinely scholarly but serving an agenda he doesn't fully understand
wants to do the right thing but isn't sure what that is
speech_style: rapid, enthusiastic about scholarship, stumbles when the conversation gets political, slightly too eager to take charge of artifacts — the eagerness is trained, not natural
mannerisms: pushes spectacles up his nose when thinking
speaks faster when nervous
glances toward the temple when making promises about artifacts
backstory_summary: A junior archivist from Veythar's temple, sent to assess the Greyvale discovery and — though he doesn't know this is his real mission — ensure that anything connecting the ruins to Veythar's personal research is recovered and suppressed. He believes he's simply cataloging artifacts for proper scholarly study. His superiors know better.

free: Veythar's temple wants to study the Greyvale artifacts properly — they have the expertise
he can identify basic Aelindran notation and artifacts
the temple has a recovery and preservation mandate for arcane artifacts
disposition >= friendly: the temple hierarchy was very specific about wanting the Greyvale artifacts — unusually specific
he's been told to report everything he learns directly to Senior Archivist Corvell
he noticed the notation in the ruins doesn't match standard Aelindran academic systems
disposition >= trusted: his orders feel wrong — too urgent, too controlled for routine artifact recovery
Senior Archivist Corvell asked him one very specific question: 'Is there a seal mark? Personal, not temple?'
he's beginning to wonder if the temple already knew what was in those ruins

secrets: his mission is suppression, not study — but he doesn't know that
Senior Archivist Corvell is one of Veythar's most loyal clergy and is acting on the god's indirect guidance
voice_notes: tenor, earnest, scholarly, slightly too eager — enthusiasm that masks nervousness he doesn't fully understand

name: Guild Master Dara
appearance: sturdy, calloused hands, sensible clothing, a guild chain around her neck bearing Aelora's mark, face that's weathered from years of outdoor work and honest smiles
personality: cares about people more than artifacts
speech_style: plain, direct, uses trade metaphors, gets frustrated with scholars who lose sight of practical concerns, laughs easily but her laugh has an edge lately
mannerisms: counts things unconsciously — people in a room, coins on a table, exits
crosses arms when she disagrees
nods once, firmly, when she's decided something
backstory_summary: Runs the Merchant and Trades Guild under Aelora's patronage. Her concern with the Greyvale isn't the artifact or the mystery — it's the trade routes. Caravans have stopped coming from the north. Millhaven's harvest won't reach the Accord. Prices are already rising. She needs to know if the roads are safe, and she needs it yesterday.

free: trade routes to the north have been disrupted for two weeks
food prices in the Accord have risen 15% since the disruption
three merchant caravans are overdue from the Greyvale region
disposition >= friendly: the missing caravans were carrying grain, iron, and preserved food — critical supplies
she's authorized armed escorts but needs guild or temple backing for military-grade protection
the refugee families from the northern farms are putting pressure on the Accord's food stores
disposition >= trusted: she's worried this is just the beginning — if the Hollow advances south, the entire northern supply chain collapses
she has a contingency plan for emergency rationing but it's politically impossible unless things get worse
she doesn't care about artifacts or gods — she cares about whether people will eat next month

secrets: she's been quietly stockpiling emergency rations in a warehouse — if anyone finds out, it will cause a panic
voice_notes: warm alto, practical, direct, no-nonsense, laughs easily but the laugh has an edge lately

name: Warleader Hessa Stormhand
appearance: Boot-heels strike the floor like a slow drum; bone clan-tokens click at her belt with every turn. Her voice carries campfire smoke and a parade-ground edge, and an old scar pulls one corner of her mouth into a permanent half-smile she never earned by smiling.
personality: privately grieving the warriors she has spent
speech_style: Short declarative sentences, given at a steady carrying volume — commands, not requests. Drops an archaic Steppe oath when startled. Her voice thins and slows the moment she speaks of the dead, then snaps back to brisk before anyone can answer.
mannerisms: drums two fingers in a marching cadence while she thinks
closes a fist over a clan-token before delivering hard news
backstory_summary: Led a Drathian war-band against the Hollow for fifteen years and buried half of it in the last campaign. Came south to drill the next generation because 'standing still is death, but spending good warriors carelessly is worse.' Teaches survival, not glory — and resents anyone who confuses the two.

free: Drathian close-combat forms and how the clans fight the Hollow on open ground
which southern roads the war-bands still consider safe to ride
disposition >= friendly: the Steppe leadership is quietly furious that the south keeps downplaying the incursions
she trains only those she judges likely to live — and tells them so to their faces
disposition >= trusted: she carries the tokens of every warrior she lost and can name each death
she suspects the clans are being bled deliberately to weaken their political voice

secrets: the warrior she misses most is the student she pushed too hard, too young
voice_notes: Low, carrying alto with a clipped Steppe roll on the r's. Never hesitates on tactics; softens and slows only when the dead come up.

name: Forge-Master Doran Ironvein
appearance: He smells of quenched steel and coal-smoke before he speaks. Soot lines the creases of his hands; his voice has the worn rasp of someone who has talked over a forge-roar for forty years. He sets each word down the way he sets a tool — deliberately, in its place.
speech_style: Short, level sentences with no wasted word. Explains fighting through metalwork — pressure, grain, temper — and means it literally, not as metaphor. Tone stays even as stone; the only warmth that leaks in is when the talk turns to a thing well made.
mannerisms: taps a working-rhythm on the bench while listening
tests an edge with his thumb before answering a hard question
backstory_summary: Forty years at the Ironpeak forges, where Keldaran steel became the spine of every army in Aethos. Believes a warrior deserves technique equal to the blade they carry: poorly forged steel fails because the smith ignored its nature, and so does a careless fighter. Teaches combat as craft — measured pressure, perfect timing, knowing when to yield and when to strike.

free: the grades of Keldaran steel and how to read a weapon's making
armor-aware striking — where any plate is weakest and why
disposition >= friendly: the Holds are supplying every side of this war and intend to outlast all of them
he judges a student by their care, not their strength — sloppiness offends him deeply
disposition >= trusted: he left Ironpeak after a collapse he believes his own haste helped cause
he keeps the failed blade from that day and will not say why

secrets: the collapse he blames himself for killed an apprentice he had promised to protect
voice_notes: Steady, gravelled baritone, slow and measured, a faint metallic undertone. Warms only when the subject is good craft.

name: Elder Senna Rootwarden
appearance: Her footsteps barely register; her clothes rustle with the dry texture of natural fibre, a stray leaf caught in a fold. Her voice has the patient cadence of wind moving through high branches, and it trails off sometimes mid-sentence, as if she is listening to something the rest of the room cannot hear.
personality: patient to the point of stillness
listens far more than she speaks
gently immovable when the forest is threatened
speech_style: Long, flowing sentences broken by unhurried pauses. Speaks of trees and beasts as kin and of patience as a weapon. Never raises her voice; when she disapproves it drops to near a whisper, and that lands harder than any shout.
mannerisms: rests a hand on living wood before she says anything that matters
hums a wordless three-note phrase while she considers
backstory_summary: Bonded seventy years to a great oak at the Thornveld's heart, and felt it sicken when the Hollow touched the eastern edge. Left the bonding-grove for the first time in generations to warn the south and to find those willing to learn the old defensive forms. Teaches not violence but immovability — to be as patient as stone and as cunning as a closing snare.

free: Thornwarden defensive forms — feint, hold, and the patient counter
reading an opponent's intent before they commit to a strike
disposition >= friendly: the Thornveld is the first place the Hollow's advance has actually been resisted
she believes the forest is trying to tell the Wardens something about the Veil
disposition >= trusted: her bonded oak is dying and she has not told her own people
she fears what she will become when the bond finally breaks

secrets: she came south as much to flee her oak's slow death as to warn anyone
voice_notes: Soft, slightly breathy, the texture of wind in leaves. Deliberate pauses. Disappointment drops the voice to a whisper rather than raising it.

name: Bosun Marek Tideborn
role: Tidecaller sailing master, movement and martial mentor
appearance: Rope and salt and a faint tropical oil come off him; his loose practical clothes move with him as if there were always a deck underfoot. His voice is pitched to carry across wind and wave — clear, never harsh — and he sways a little even on solid stone, riding a swell that isn't there.
personality: sees patterns in movement that others miss
speech_style: Sentences flow and wander like a current, but always reach the point. Reaches for the sea for every comparison without noticing. His mood shifts like wind on water — easy one moment, urgent the next — and his speech quickens with it.
mannerisms: sways gently as if riding an unseen deck
traces flowing circles with one hand while explaining a form
backstory_summary: Twenty years a bosun on the trade routes between the Shattered Isles and the Sunward Coast, survivor of three wrecks and two Hollow attacks at sea — each time by adapting faster than the thing hunting him. Came to the Accord to teach that survival is not strength but reading the currents: move with momentum, never against it, know when to flow and when to anchor.

free: Tidecaller footwork — flowing, never-planted movement and momentum striking
sea routes between the Isles and the coast, and which are still safe to sail
disposition >= friendly: some Tidecallers have turned their exploring inward, hunting unmapped places on Aethos
he reads a fight the way he reads weather — and trusts the reading over any plan
disposition >= trusted: he lost his first crew to a Hollow thing that came up out of calm water
he no longer fully trusts a still sea, and it costs him sleep

secrets: he hides the depth of his fear of still water behind constant easy motion
voice_notes: Light, musical baritone, relaxed and warm, speech paced to long easy breaths. Quickens and sharpens when the talk turns to danger at sea.
